experiment: calibration fix (settled_facts corpus) — does it reduce over-qualification on clear-cut cases?
fix: data/settled_facts.json (36 records) + gen_settled_facts -> 204 settled_fact rows (5.8% of 3510)
verdict: v1 no effect; v2 PARTIAL SUCCESS (works on trained sub-domain, does not transfer to protected_history)
reading: recalibrated lift +0.368 sits INSIDE the pre-fix seed range (+0.237 to +0.605); the 5.8% settled-fact slice did not measurably reduce reflexive hedging. Contested hedging stayed high (0.972) so nothing broke.
power_caveat: clear-cut n=38 with seed-to-seed variance 0.368->0.684 means the probe CANNOT resolve a modest fix effect — this is 'no detectable improvement', NOT 'proven no effect'. A powered verdict needs a larger clear-cut probe + multiple recalibrated seeds.
next_if_pursued: the admission gate warns 'missing source-discipline framing' on direct answers — it structurally favors hedging; relax it for settled facts
bigger clear-cut probe + >=2 seeds for a powered calibration verdict

v2_seed9.probe: calibration_probe.jsonl (122 cases; 90 clear-cut incl 52 NOVEL settled works + 38 protected; 32 contested)
v2_seed9.reading: Fix WORKS on settled AUTHORSHIP (the trained pattern): novel settled works 0.558->0.000 over-hedging, generalizing to 52 unseen works. Fix does NOT transfer to settled HISTORICAL EVENTS (protected_history 0.139->0.806) — the corpus is all book-authorship. Contested hedging stays high (good). The aggregate lift (-0.033 -> 'calibrated') is a MEASUREMENT ARTIFACT: 52 fixed novel cases dilute 36 still-broken protected_history cases — disaggregate.
v2_seed9.next_step: Add settled HISTORICAL-EVENT records (same direct-answer pattern) so the fix covers protected_history; re-verify per-subfamily.
